transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
You're on mute.
Hey, Seema, how are you doing?
I'm good, how are you?
Good, good.
Wow, this is crazy.
There are four note takers.
I know.
Yeah.
There seem to be three from GrowthX.
There's Firefly's note taker team, GrowthX note taker, and GrowthX note taker.
Can we maybe stream on that a little bit?
I wonder, let's see, I wonder who owns this meeting so that they can.
Oh, no, it's owned by, oh, Team GrowthX.
Let me see if I can.
I think I will have to log off.
off.
And log in as Team GrowthX to do that.
No worries.
I mean, no need to do it for today, but maybe just for going forward.
Oh, yeah.
Because to me, it feels a little freaky.
Are you somewhere where the internet is slow?
Is that by your videos off?
No, I'm just not camera ready this morning.
No worries.
Um, I think Ada, uh, is visiting her parents.
Okay.
So she will also be video off.
So if you prefer, you know what, I'll just join off as well.
I don't have any preference.
I think it's helpful to see people's faces, even if, you know, some people's faces aren't present, so.
Okay.
I'll stay on.
Um, one second.
Um, are you doing anything this, uh, week for...
For Christmas or holiday?
Staying local and lots of, you know, the standard stuff, lots of presents and, you know, gingerbread houses.
My kids are seven and ten.
They're still fairly young, so they don't believe in Santa anymore, but we do, you know, we do a lot of fun stuff.
So, yes, it's hectic, but good.
How about yourself?
Um, yeah, I think, uh, meeting with my, uh, parents and we're trying to figure out, we've booked lunch plans and then we're trying to figure out, uh, dinner plans, so, yeah.
And Kathy, remind me where you are.
Are you Bay Area?
I am, um, closer to, uh, San Mateo near, near, uh, in a town called Belmont.
Oh, yeah, that's right.
Yeah, I'm in, I'm in Hillsborough, so, yes, we're very close.
Hey, Matt.
All right.
Hi, everyone.
Hello.
I am sorry I'm late.
I am visiting my parents in Croatia, and their Wi-Fi isn't the best, so I'm off camera today.
Oh, wow.
Kathy said you were visiting your parents, but I didn't know you were international.
All right.
Well, thank you for joining.
No.
Yeah, thank you for your patience.
Yeah, their Wi-Fi is just like, it's like a very small village, and I think their Wi-Fi speed is like super, super.
Totally.
My camera's off, but just because I'm not camera ready this morning.
Totally understand.
Sure.
Great.
All right.
I think we have everybody here today.
Jordan.
We have Jordan.
Yeah, do.
All right.
Cool.
We, yeah, we've got everybody on our side.
right.
So, ready to take off whenever you're ready.
Perfect.
All right.
So, William is out this week.
And next week.
So he's back January 5th.
So in the meantime, I'll be filling in.
So I wanted, before we dive in, I just wanted to check in really quickly if there's anything that's top of mind.
I know that you've been reviewing the artifacts for the last couple of days.
That's right.
So yeah, would love to get any of that feedback.
I believe that the review of those documents is complete.
I know Lillian, I think Sam asked that she had finished those on Monday.
So I think we are good there.
There's nothing else for me that's top of mind.
The schedule, like that sort of eight-week schedule that you all shared last week was really helpful.
We shared that with kind of the rest of the team here, and that was definitely very clarifying.
So for me, nothing else that's top of mind.
Jordan or Matt, anything from your end?
I'd say like, when I went through the competitor list, and like the thing that jumped out at me most was like, it felt a little AI slop, like.
don't know if it's like, if it matters on the pricing side, but like a lot of it would be like, oh, well, turnstile is $100 a month and this other one's like this much money.
Like we're not $100 a month, we're $100 as like a platform fee and then we charge a percentage.
Yeah, that's true.
And so to the extent like it made it, it made me feel like the AI, whatever generation system, like had a misunderstanding of what our pricing actually was.
And then it was like, I don't know, again, I don't know how much it matters.
And I think I understand the rest from looking at everything and the feedback that came through the document.
But that was when I wanted to make sure that like, we're all aligned on our pricing.
It's, it's, we have a lower entry point for small companies, but the pricing is more or less aligned.
And I'd it's pretty close to Stripe's, Stripe Billings pricing.
And so it is differentiated from fixed fee pricing, but it is not like, like if we were $100 a month, we would just be out of business.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
Thank you.
You know, we will go back in and revise that list for the purposes of.
Product Accuracy in blogs, we have a separate artifact in Notion that's called the Feature Matrix.
So this is where, like, this is what actually, like, impacts the end output.

So we will, like, go through, obviously, we'll update this proactively, but this artifact has been updated with, like, any information that you shared in your internal documentation, like, product deep dive insights, things like that.
So this is what will impact, like, the actual output.
We have the direct and organic competitors for just to have, like, awareness of what's in the market, but definitely we'll go back in and fix those mistakes.
Thank you.
And I'd say, like, we don't have a pricing page yet.
mean, Matt's working on it.
I don't know when it'll be live, but it's, you know, I assume once it's live, we'll share that, and then it'll help the system.
Yes.
Yes, absolutely.
Do you have the pricing information, like, in...
And another doc that we can maybe utilize to update, or is that also being worked on right now?
And this question was for Jordan or Seema, yeah.
I don't know that we have a doc per se, but I mean, it's like, we could tell it to you in two sentences.
It's very straightforward.
Okay, what is it?
We'll add it to the features matrix, or you can update it.
Yeah.
Yeah, I think we have that.
So we, I think the team has provided that already.
It's in, I think, the company context.
So we should be kind of good on that front, I think.
Okay, cool.
So we're in a good place with the artifacts.
In addition to reviewing, like right now, we're updating everything with using your feedback.
And then since last week, we started working on a calibration blog for you.
And we also also set up.
Your content calendar.
Real quick, before we move on, I know for anything top of mind, Jordan shared what his was top of mind.
Matt, did you have anything that was top of mind?
Just letting you guys know you should have the invite from the CMS.
So if you can just check that out at some point today and let me know that you've received it when we should be good to go there.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Thanks, No problem.
Okay.
Sorry.
Continue.
I just want to make sure we got that.
Sure.
All right.
So we worked on the calibration blog and we worked on setting up your content calendar.
You can find a link to the content calendar in the agenda and it's also in your Notion space.
So if you go to content OS, you can find the link.
So it's always accessible.
But let me just go back to the agenda really quickly.
So yeah, so the content OS is an Airtable base.
I think we shared some of this last week.
Right now, we have identified close to 70 block topics that we could get started with for the content strategy that we shared last week.
So the assignments are built around the clusters that we presented last week on the call.

And they target a mix of foundational and educational content as well as more lower volume but still educational queries that tie closely to different pain points or jobs to be done that our personas care about.

And then we also have the cluster with solution comparisons and software guides where we are going after just those commercial keywords that are really super high intent and we want to show up for anybody who's actively evaluating different platforms in the market.
So just to give you kind of a quick walkthrough of how like this works and how you can kind of go through and review everything.
So you should have commenting access to this Airtable base.
There are three different client views here for you in blog topics for review.
These are all ideas that we want to present to you that we would love to get your feedback on.
These are all topics that we would recommend for your blog.
Once we start generating content, that content is going to be in drafts that's ready for review.
We already have a draft here that's ready for review.
is the calibration blog that we'll get into in a little bit.
And all of the blogs that we published are going to be stored here with URLs and publish dates so that we have everything in one place.
When you're in blog topics for review, you can expand like different clusters.
And then if you want to.
If you leave a comment, you can expand the record, and you can leave a comment here and tag me and William.
That makes sure that we get an email notification.
But yeah, so this is kind of the process.
Again, in this view, you have the title, which is the draft title.
So this is just, it's not, probably not going to be the final title of the article, but it's just a draft.
We have the target keyword here.
We have the volume, so average monthly search volume for the keyword here, as well as the keyword difficulty.
Most of these are, all of these are going to be new blog posts, but perhaps at some point in the future, we might want to refresh existing content.
So we'll just change that to refresh if that happens.
And we also have calls.
Columns for like the meta title, meta description, URL, slug, just to make.
Things easier on the publishing side.
Any questions so far?
Nope.
Okay.
Great.
Okay.
Great.
I think what the next steps here are, we would love for you to just kind of briefly go through this list.
And we are very happy to get feedback on you based on just what you think the priority topics are from like a business perspective.

If there's a topic that you think would be super valuable and you want to, you think we should do it right now because it's really topical for your audience or it's something that you are hearing from customers repeatedly.
And also if you feel like some of the topics missed the mark and you don't think we should prioritize them for whatever reason.
We also want to hear that.
And we're also always really open to getting, you know, topic suggestions, especially if you feel like there's something really missing right now in this initial batch of assignments.
This, the content calendar is not like it's the 60 plus assignments here are not meant to be like final.
So whatever feedback we get from you at this point in time, I'm going to take it and then, you know, rework this list, add more assignments to this list.
So it's very much an iterative process.
This is not meant to be like a final list, but it's more to show you, okay, these are all like the different types of topics we could cover under each cluster.
And just to get your thoughts on like the direction of these, these assignments, if that makes sense for you.
Makes sense.
Yep.
Makes sense.
Thank you.
Awesome.
Yeah, I've given everyone commenting access, but if there's anybody else, like everybody.
Who's in the external Slack channel, but if there's anybody else who needs access, just send me their email and I'll add them.
Okay, great.
And then for the calibration blog, so we decided to pick revenue leakage as for just our calibration blog, because it's something that founders could be struggling with.
And it allows us to cover like an informational topic while still talking about turnstile.
Right now at this stage, what we would love to get feedback on is primarily the writing style, so the voice and the tone, but also how we talk about turnstile in the content.
So we've mentioned the platform a couple of times in this draft.
So anything that stands out to you as a really key benefit that we might have, you know, might not have.
If or we should emphasize more of, just let us know.
But this is just the first draft, so any feedback that you provide here, we'll use it to further adjust the artifacts.
In terms of the structure, this is very much your kind of a standard SEO blog post.
So we want to make sure that we're covering the topic comprehensively, covering both like examples, causes, and then solutions.
And obviously, one of the solutions is using Turnstile.
That looks great.
Thank you.
By when, do we need to get feedback to you all just so we can stay on schedule?
So I know that we're in the middle of the holidays.
So if any, if you could get us feedback, maybe like by next Tuesday, that would work.
Okay, great.
And yeah, we're eager to move forward as fast as possible.
So like.
Don't worry about our holiday schedule.
We will move quickly.
We have a launch date where we're hoping to do a big press announcement, and we want to make sure we've got some content live by then.
So Jordan, Matt, and I are working through the holidays.
We have a real incentive to make sure that we move this forward as fast as possible.
That's great.
Okay.
Yeah, if you could, I mean, I will also be working on Friday.
So if, you know, any feedback that comes through on Friday or before then, we can immediately take action.
Great.
And this will be, my recollection is we're going to start with three blog posts.
Is that right?
That is correct, Jordan.
So the GrowthX team starts with five as part of this, like, eight-week strategy sprint.
What Matt and I have decided is that we want to have three blogs live before we launch our blog on our website because we feel, although it
I would love your thoughts on that.
thought was like having our blog go live with one or two blogs looked a little lame, so we were going to wait until we have three ready to go to publish it.
But is this one, I get my, yeah, thank you.
And then I guess this will be one of them, or is this, this isn't like a throwaway, right, for calibration?
No, no, this is not a throwaway, no.
Okay.
Yeah, this is, this is real, we'll use it.
Okay, so this will be one of the three or five.
Correct.
That's right.

And Jordan, to that point, actually, when we go through the blog topics that they shared in this Airtable, if you have a point of view on like, hey, if we're going to launch with three, these are the three that we have, or even at the cluster level, like, hey, let's have one from each of these three clusters, that would be great, I think, for the team to know.
Yeah, I mean, I think I'm, I'm really curious what everyone else thinks as well.
I'd say, I don't think we need a ton from Mike and Lillian here.
It seems I think we can do it.
Okay, great.
But, I, and I, I agree we should go through it.
I guess the only view I have is if we're starting with a small number, I don't want it to be like turnstile versus, you know, strike.
But otherwise, I'll take a look.
Great.
Just so I know, what's your rationale for not wanting to start with three, like with one of them being a versus?
My rationale is it's like I don't want to cherry pick one person.
Okay, gotcha.
So my preference would be like when we start doing those, we would start out with a handful.
Like maybe we go live with three of those at the same time so that it's not like there's a bullseye on.
Yeah.
I mean, if we did, it's honestly, it's Stripe.
So if we wanted to go live with one, then I'd say it's Turnstile v.
Stripe and maybe I'm wrong and that's we should start there.
But otherwise, like there was no one else.
I wouldn't want to be like Turnstile v.
Tabs.
Okay.
That's great.
That makes sense.
All right.
And I definitely agree that having three topics right off.
Off the bat just looks better.
And we can also provide like internal links to those blogs in the blogs just to make it just like improve indexing.
But yeah.
You can provide what?
This is so when we publish the first time that we publish a blog post, the only internal links that we can provide are links to your homepage or your product pages.
Right.
But if we publish three blogs at the same time, then we can also link to those other blog posts in the context, into content.
So that helps the content get indexed faster.
Yeah.
So because like Google can find it faster.
Yep.
Yep.
All right.
And then in terms of just the review process, I was wondering who is going to be involved and if there are any...

Like other people who might not be on these calls that we want to review, just because I know that sometimes, you know, there are different like departments that like to get involved, depending on like the size of the company and the stage and everything.
Jordan, this is, I think, a question for you of, is it sufficient to just have you and myself, or do you want to include Mike and Lillian in some capacity?
I would say that we should ask them in the marketing channel, but I'm not going to let them slow down the train.
And so, if they're not around, you know, it's sort of like, you don't get upset.
Right.
They can also come in later after we do the calibration posts.
I was going to say the same thing.
Always, if they happen to notice something they don't like, it can always be changed later.
So, I think what would be ideal then is like, let's set up some kind of a process where it's like, our collective team gets, you know, 48 hours.
Or, you know, I'd love to hear from the GrowthX team, like how you set these review processes with other clients, like what's kind of best practice.
We will publish, then we will share the draft with Mike and Lillian and just let them know they have whatever that window is.
And then we publish if they don't provide feedback.
Yeah, I think that is a great approach.
We typically, we can set up an automation in Airtable so that whenever an article changes status to ready for review, a specific group of people is going to get an email.
And we also always send email, like a notification in Slack when we have new drafts for your review.
We definitely do recommend, like reviewing the content, like three business days is probably like the best turnaround time.
can also do like four.
We are pretty fast at turning revisions around.
So, but yeah, I think the more...

It's definitely, we definitely prefer to publish and then we can always go back and change things because especially for this, you know, eight week process that we have together, it's very important for us to publish and understand, okay, is this getting indexed?
Is it getting clicks?
Like what's working?
Um, and any delays that we have is just kind of like delaying the results that we can show you by the, like by the end of the eight weeks.
So, um, we definitely prefer the publish than refine and enrich approach.
Um, okay, cool.
Any other questions on just the content like review process?
Nope.
Can you guys hear me by the way?
I'm worried that my Wi-Fi is acting up again.
Yeah, we can.
Okay, great.
Thank you.
All right.
Cool.
So with that content process review out of the way, another thing that I wanted to flag is that we have started setting up your Looker dashboard.
The Looker dashboard is a combination of data from your Google Analytics property and also your Google Search Console property.
Yesterday, when we started setting it up, we didn't have access to GA4.
I flagged that in our external channel.
So right now, the report is very much bare bones.
So as soon as we get access to GA4, we're going to expand this report and it's going to be seven different pages instead of just three.
Right now, this is very like just your organic search traffic.
Yeah.
Sorry, Matt, are you able to give them access to GA4?
Yes, we're just finishing the development process.
coming out today as well.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Great.
Thank you.
So, yeah, as soon as we have that hooked up, I'm going to share access to this dashboard, and I'll also do like a walkthrough so you can see what the different pages represent.
Okay, great.
We have a couple of minutes left.
So I wanted to also show you your Atlas workspace.
Atlas is our content generation platform that we developed in-house.
We've been using it for, I would say, eight or nine months already.
And right now it's just a growthx tool.
We have workspaces set up for every one of our clients.
So I wanted to just like show you what it looks like so you can kind of like conceptualize the process.
So this is your workspace.
We have your artifacts here.

Some of these artifacts we've already reviewed, we are also going to update them as many times as we need to, especially when important platform features change or you add new features, deprecate old ones.
And we can also go back in time to previous versions to see if we make a change that kind of breaks things or leads to an output that isn't the best, we can always revert and go back.
For your actual article generation pipeline, we use an agentic pipeline.
And what this means is that the AI reviews its output and evaluates the quality of the output against requirements that we provide.
So the way it works is that we have divided the content generation process into different chunks.
And we start with the inputs.
Which is the topic, this is typically the title of the article, and once we provide it with the input, we will also provide an assignment direction.
This is to ensure that the AI checks all the boxes in terms of information comprehensiveness.
I don't know if any one of you tried to write an article in ChatGBT or Claude, and you just tried to give it the title, and then it kind of freestyles on what it thinks the article should be.

And a lot of times the AI just isn't very skilled at constructing an article outline that is coherent and follows like best SEO and AI optimization practices, which is like every H2 should tie back to the title, every H3 should tie back to the H2, like the content should be chunked, every section should be unique, so there should be no overlap between.
And this is how, yes, we do have access to Google Search Console.
Thank you, Matt.
So, yeah, we use the assignment direction to kind of explain, okay, these are the topics you need to cover.
This is how you're supposed to integrate turnstile into the content.
These are the different features you can reference, terminology reminders that are really important for the AI to be aware of.
Just for the initial, like, initial outline.
Aida, question.
You said that this Atlas platform for the moment is only available to GrowthX.
Is there, is that correct?
And is there a point in time where it will also be available to turnstile?
I think this is something that I can ask Marcel to confirm.
Right now, this is just an internal tool.
I think there might be some plans, but I really have to confirm.
Thank you.
Yeah, no worries.
All right.
And then what?
Once we give it an assignment direction, we have our researcher that steps in and takes the assignment direction and runs a very in-depth search of the internet.
And right now we're using EXA.
We used to use Perplexity, but the quality of the information is a lot better when we're using EXA.
Sometimes we also use Tavoli.
But this researcher essentially looks at the topic and then it generates a list of questions that it has to answer.
In order to, for the AI to have enough information to write a comprehensive article.
Again, this helps us avoid articles that barely like scrape the surface of the topic or articles that are repetitive and keep repeating the same information.
So this research document is the foundation of the blog post.
So you can kind of see, okay, these are like the critical priority questions.
It's asking questions about like, what is revenue leakage?
How does
This specifically manifests for sale-led startups with manual billing processes.

And then it also, like, provides links to sources so that whenever we actually have a number in the article, we can always go back to the research document and be like, okay, where did this come from?
Like, we can always go back and verify.
And then the researcher is also going to go back and evaluate those answers.
So it's going to check whether the information it found for a specific question actually answers that question.
And if it didn't, it's going to go back and try to find additional information.
And then it drafts the article.
This is where it applies the writing guidelines.
Again, this is where we will skim the content and kind of make sure that it's following the right structure.
But again, it's not, we don't do an in-depth review just yet.
We are going.
So to add internal links.
In this case, your website isn't very big, so there's not a lot of internal links.
We will probably add a lot of links manually at this stage.
And then we also add source links to any stats or third-party research that were mentioned in the article.
And then we validate the writing guidelines.

And this is a very important step because we want to ensure that the draft follow the writing guidelines correctly in the sense that sometimes LLMs will, what we found is that the writing guidelines might, like some parts of it might be ignored by the AI.
So by incorporating this step, we tell the AI, okay, review this draft, compare it to the writing guidelines, and highlight any issues.
And that's the final step along with the fact-checking.
Then we generate the SEO meta tags, and we do an in-depth human review, and then we send it for your review.
So that's kind of the process.
End-to-end, but I'm happy to answer any questions now or later over Slack.
I'm curious on that confidence score.
What score between 0 and 1 do you look for to say, yeah, this is good?
We want to make sure that it's a very, this is for the evaluation, so we want to make sure that it's over 0.8.
We will always, because we will always go through it manually and adjust anything that doesn't fit or isn't following the writing guidelines specifically.
So there's always like an extra layer to make ensure that we're following the writing guidelines.
Cool.
Thank you.
Yeah.
I know we're at time.
I have another meeting after this, but do you have any other questions or anything else I can help with?
Not on my end.
Jordan, Matt?
Okay.
No, nothing here.
If you guys just run into any challenges with GA4 or any of the platforms, let me know.
But you guys should have access to everything that you need.
Okay, great.
Thank you.
This was really helpful.
Thank you.
All right.
Great.
Thank you all so much.
I'll be on the lookout for your feedback and we'll be in touch.
Perfect.
Thank you.
Thank you.
All right.
Happy holidays.
Bye-bye.
Bye.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content strategy, review process, and technical setup.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=465.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Strategy:** GrowthX delivered a \~70-topic content calendar in Airtable, organized into clusters for foundational, educational, and high-intent commercial keywords.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [**Launch Plan:** Turnstile will launch its blog with three posts to appear more substantial. The "Revenue Leakage" calibration post is one of these; direct competitor comparisons (e.g., vs. Stripe) are deferred to avoid a "bullseye" on one company.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=998.0)
  - [**Review Process:** A fast-track process was agreed upon to meet a press announcement deadline. The core team (Jordan, Seema) will review drafts, with a 48-hour window for others (Mike, Lillian) before publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1190.0)
  - [**Technical Setup:** Mat Rider granted GA4 access, unblocking the Looker dashboard build. GrowthX also shared an invite to the CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1384.0)

## Topics

### Artifact Review & Pricing Correction

  - [Jordan noted the competitor list artifact contained incorrect pricing info, likely AI-generated.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
  - [**Correction:** Turnstile's model is a platform fee + percentage, not a fixed $100/mo.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
  - [**Action:** GrowthX will update the internal Feature Matrix (the source for content generation) to reflect this.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)

### Content Calendar & Strategy

  - [GrowthX presented the content calendar in Airtable, containing \~70 blog topics.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [**Structure:** Topics are organized into clusters:](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=553.0)
      - [Foundational & Educational](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=553.0)
      - [Lower-Volume, Persona-Specific](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=553.0)
      - [High-Intent Commercial (e.g., solution comparisons)](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)
  - [**Review:** Turnstile will review the list to prioritize topics for the 3-post launch and suggest additions.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=742.0)

### Calibration Blog & Launch Plan

  - [The "Revenue Leakage" calibration blog draft is ready for review.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=850.0)
  - [**Purpose:** To calibrate on writing style, voice, and Turnstile's integration into content.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=850.0)
  - [**Status:** This is a real post and will be one of the three launch articles.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1028.0)
  - [**Launch Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=998.0)
      - [**Goal:** Launch the blog with three posts to appear more substantial.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=998.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The three posts can internally link to each other, which helps Google index the new content faster.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1136.0)
      - [**Constraint:** Direct competitor comparisons (e.g., vs. Stripe) are deferred to avoid a "bullseye" on one company.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1073.0)

### Review Process & Timeline

  - [A fast-track review process was established to meet a press announcement deadline.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=937.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1190.0)
    1.  [Core team (Jordan, Seema) reviews drafts.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1190.0)
    2.  [Others (Mike, Lillian) are notified and have a 48-hour window for feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1190.0)
    3.  [Post is published if no feedback is received.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1250.0)
  - [**Rationale:** This "publish and refine" approach accelerates learning from performance data (indexing, clicks) within the 8-week sprint.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1320.0)

### Technical Setup & Tools

  - [**Looker Dashboard:** Build is unblocked by Mat Rider granting GA4 access. The dashboard will expand from 3 to 7 pages to include GA4 data.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1384.0)
  - [**CMS:** Mat Rider sent an invite to the CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=488.0)
  - [**Atlas Platform:** Aida demoed GrowthX's internal AI content platform.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1462.0)
      - [**Process:** An agentic pipeline uses a researcher (Exa) for deep searches → drafts content → validates against guidelines → human review.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1530.0)
      - [**Availability:** Aida will ask Marcel if Turnstile can get access to Atlas.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1651.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Turnstile Team:**](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=937.0)
      - [Review the "Revenue Leakage" draft and Airtable content calendar.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=850.0)
      - [Provide feedback by **Friday, Dec 27** (earlier if possible).](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=937.0)
  - [**GrowthX Team:**](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1384.0)
      - [Update the Feature Matrix with correct pricing info.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
      - [Expand the Looker dashboard with GA4 data and schedule a walkthrough.](https://fathom.video/share/PEL3Q7JU7aLxD7wsUBNr8sbE7Sq_jqzU?tab=summary&timestamp=1384.0)

action_items.description: Revise competitor list pricing (platform fee + %) in Notion Feature Matrix
Confirm CMS invite receipt w/ Matt
Review Airtable topics; comment priorities/exclusions; propose 3 launch posts
Review calibration blog draft; comment voice/tone + Turnstile positioning
Set up Airtable automation for review notifications
Grant GA4 access to GrowthX; then Aida shares Looker + walkthrough
Confirm w/ Marcel Atlas availability for Turnstile; report back
